questions.question: Who was the Happy Prince?
questions.answer: The Happy Prince was a statue placed on a tall column in the city.

questions.question: How did the statue of the Happy Prince look?
questions.answer: It was covered with gold leaves and had sapphire eyes and a ruby on the sword.

questions.question: Why did the swallow stay behind when the other swallows went to Egypt?
questions.answer: The swallow stayed behind because he had fallen in love with a reed.

questions.question: Where did the swallow spend the night in the city?
questions.answer: He spent the night between the feet of the Happy Prince.

questions.question: Why was the Happy Prince crying?
questions.answer: He was crying because he could see the suffering and misery of the people in the city.

questions.question: Why did the prince ask the swallow to take the ruby to the seamstress?
questions.answer: The prince wanted to help the poor seamstress and her sick child.

questions.question: What problem did the seamstress’s child have?
questions.answer: The child was suffering from fever.

questions.question: How did the swallow help the sick boy?
questions.answer: He fanned the boy’s forehead with his wings.

questions.question: Why did the swallow feel warm after helping the poor?
questions.answer: He felt warm because he had done a good deed.

questions.question: Who was the young man helped by the prince?
questions.answer: He was a poor playwright.

questions.question: Why was the playwright unable to finish his work?
questions.answer: He was too cold and hungry to write.

questions.question: What did the prince sacrifice to help the playwright?
questions.answer: He sacrificed one of his sapphire eyes.

questions.question: Who was the little girl helped by the prince?
questions.answer: She was a poor match girl.

questions.question: Why was the match girl crying?
questions.answer: Her matches had fallen in the gutter and she had no money to take home.

questions.question: What did the swallow promise the blind prince?
questions.answer: He promised to stay with the prince always.

questions.question: What did the prince ask the swallow to do with the gold leaves?
questions.answer: He asked him to give them to the poor.

questions.question: What did the swallow see when he flew over the city?
questions.answer: He saw rich people enjoying themselves and poor people suffering.

questions.question: Why did the swallow grow weak in winter?
questions.answer: Because the weather was very cold and he had little food.

questions.question: What did the swallow do before dying?
questions.answer: He kissed the prince on the lips.

questions.question: What happened to the prince’s lead heart?
questions.answer: It broke into two pieces.

questions.question: Who ordered the statue to be pulled down?
questions.answer: The Mayor ordered it.

questions.question: Why did the Mayor consider the statue useless?
questions.answer: Because it had lost its beauty and jewels.

questions.question: What happened to the statue after it was removed?
questions.answer: It was melted in a furnace.

questions.question: What were the two precious things found by the angel?
questions.answer: The lead heart and the dead swallow.

questions.question: What is the main message of the story?
questions.answer: Kindness and sacrifice are more valuable than wealth.
